offensive.name: Living Weaponry
offensive.description: Turn body into blades, tendrils, and horrors — hardening sinew into weapons until the caster themselves becomes something biology never intended.
tiers: Create biological weapons — hardened bone spikes, veins twisted into whips. Your unarmed strikes now deal 1d8 sinew damage.
Gain multiattack (2) with unarmed strikes or body weapons. Targets struck must succeed on a Constitution save or be grappled by muscle fibers extending from the strike.
Grow a temporary monstrous feature — tentacle limb, quilled back, or acid gland. Gain one biological trait (breath weapon, defensive spine, or burrowing movement) for 1 hour.
Enter the Flesh Ascendant state for 1 minute — you count as an aberration, gain temporary HP equal to your level × 20, and your natural weapons count as magical +5.

supportive.name: Regenerative Twist
supportive.description: Accelerate or hijack the body's repair process — closing wounds and regrowing limbs with the same motion that, pointed differently, turns regeneration cancerous.
tiers: A target you touch heals 1d6 HP or takes 1d6 sinew damage (your choice). This healing cannot affect undead or constructs.
Reattach limbs, close deep wounds, or spur cellular regeneration — removing 1 Wound per round or regrowing a minor body part over 1 hour.
As part of casting, mutate a creature's organ — the target must make a Constitution save or gain 1 Injury as their own biology works against them.
Redirect regeneration violently — a regenerating creature instead bloats, grows tumors, or collapses, taking necrotic + sinew damage equal to your level as their immortality turns cancerous.

control.name: Meatshaper's Grasp
control.description: Reshape the living flesh — twisting limbs and dislocating joints before eventually replacing entire body parts with something better or worse.
tiers: The target's muscles or limbs twist unnaturally — they have disadvantage on Strength or Dexterity checks (your choice) for 1 round.
Alter a limb's function — dislocate a knee, reduce grip, or hyperextend a joint. The target suffers −1 AC, drops one held item, or takes sinew damage equal to half your level.
Reshape flesh into armor or weapons on a willing creature — granting temporary natural armor (AC = 20 + Constitution modifier) or a meaty weapon dealing 4d8 sinew damage (versatile) for 1 hour.

Create or replace entire body parts — granting natural weapons, extra limbs, tentacles, or organic mutations at DM discretion. Each replacement costs HP or Cognition fatigue equal to the complexity of the change.

control.name: Flesh Memory
control.description: Scar the body with truths that last — embedding trauma into tissue until wounds trigger hallucinations and living organs carry spells indefinitely.
tiers: Mark a creature's flesh with a symbolic wound or memory rune. They have disadvantage on Deception checks against the truth you etched for 24 hours.
Embed a memory in flesh — when the creature is next wounded, they suffer hallucinations of past trauma and must succeed on a Wisdom save or be Stunned for 1 round.
Mark yourself with Resonant Flesh — while concentrating, you can see through the eyes or feel the pain of any creature whose flesh you have marked.

Create a Flesh Anchor — a living organ made of your own tissue that can store a spell, memory, or emotional Cognition. It may be grafted into another willing creature, persisting indefinitely until removed.

control.name: Organic Dominion
control.description: Command flesh as a sovereign force — stripping reactions, causing nerve pain, and eventually puppeting the paralyzed or the dead as extensions of your own body.
tiers: Command or repulse one creature with a natural body (not constructs or oozes) — on a failed Charisma save they lose their next reaction.
Cause phantom nerve pain in an unused part of the body — the target takes 1d8 sinew damage and must succeed on a Constitution save or drop Concentration.
Dominate the body — forcibly bend limbs or lock muscles. On a failed save, the target cannot move or use one specific limb for 1 round.

Puppet a corpse or paralyzed creature for 1 minute — using its body as an extension of your own. You act through it with its physical stats and your mental actions, sharing its senses while the puppeting lasts.

complementEffects.name: Exposed Nerve
complementEffects.description: The primary effect leaves something raw and open — the next source of damage fires every nerve in the affected area at once for amplified sinew pain.
complementEffects.effect: On a hit or failed saving throw against the primary effect, the target's pain receptors are left hypersensitive — the next time they take damage before the end of your next turn, that damage deals +1d8 sinew bonus damage.
upgrades: The hypersensitivity lingers for 1 minute, the bonus damage increases to +2d8, and each time the bonus triggers the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Verum DC or have disadvantage on their next attack roll.

The bonus damage increases to +3d8, and while the hypersensitivity persists the target has disadvantage on Concentration checks — every incidental impact sending a cascade of amplified sensation through them.

complementEffects.name: Flesh Knit
complementEffects.description: Biology accelerates where it is needed most — minor wounds closing of their own accord as the body's repair process is briefly redirected toward an ally.
complementEffects.effect: When the primary effect resolves, one ally of your choice within 30 feet regains hit points equal to your spellcasting modifier + your proficiency bonus as minor wounds close of their own accord.
upgrades: The healing increases to twice your spellcasting modifier + your proficiency bonus, and the ally also removes one condition affecting them as the biological surge pushes the ailment out.
You may choose two allies to receive this benefit, and each also removes one Wound — the flesh knitting past surface damage into deeper repair.

complementEffects.name: Muscle Lock
complementEffects.description: The body betrays itself — muscles seizing involuntarily, stripping speed and coordination before the target can understand what their own flesh is doing.
complementEffects.effect: When the primary effect hits or a target fails their saving throw, that target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Verum DC or have their muscles seize involuntarily — their movement speed is reduced by 15 feet and they have disadvantage on Strength and Dexterity checks until the end of their next turn.
upgrades: On a failed save, the target also cannot take the Dash action and has disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of their next turn — their body fighting every deliberate movement.

Creatures that fail are also unable to use reactions until the end of their next turn, and any attempt to cast a spell with somatic components requires a successful Constitution saving throw against your Verum DC first — their hands too locked to gesture cleanly.
